summary: High‑energy 11/24/78 FM broadcast captures a scorching Dead show (Jack Straw, Sugaree, Loser, Estimated) with solid audience sound despite a mixed mix and Jerry’s sick vocals.
review: Even though Jerry Garcia was battling pneumonia and his voice sounded ragged, the band turned in one of ’78’s hottest performances – tight Jack Straw and Sugaree openings, an out‑of‑this‑world Loser, a must‑hear Estimated, and stellar Friend of the Devil/Friend of the Devil. The FM broadcast source is clear enough for most listeners (drums punchy, overall clarity good) though the mix can let drums overpower Jerry’s guitar at times.

ai_rating.rationale: The overwhelming majority of reviews are 4‑5 stars, praising the musical performance while noting only modest recording quirks; weighted rating (70 % show + 30 % recording) lands in the strong‑good range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear overall FM broadcast sound with excellent drum presence and good instrument separation. Some listeners note a slightly off‑balance mix (drums sometimes dominate Jerry’s guitar) and occasional player UI issues, but no major drop‑outs.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set launches with a smoking Jack Straw and Sugaree, setting a high bar. The second set builds momentum through Loser, Estimated, and a fiery Friend of the Devil, delivering a hot, cohesive arc despite Jerry’s vocal limitations.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Voice was croaky and nasal due to pneumonia, but his guitar work and solos were praised as anthemic and remarkable.
band_member_comments.Keys: Donna Jean’s vocals noted as cohesive; keyboard texture generally supportive but not highlighted separately.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drums sound excellent and punchy; occasional mix dominance over guitar mentioned.
